volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Ghost (Section 2.2.519b - Section 3.1.581)

sections.label: Section 2.2.519b
sections.translation: Prithee , was it you that called me ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.520
sections.original: ita me di amabunt , mortuom illum credidi
sections.translation: So may the Gods bless me , I fancied it was this dead man expostulating because you had knocked at the door . But are you still standing there , and not doing what I advise you ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.523
sections.translation: What am I to do ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.523b
sections.original: Cave respexis , fuge , atque operi caput .
sections.translation: Take care not to look back . Fly ; cover up your head !

sections.label: Section 2.2.524
sections.translation: Why don ’ t you fly ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.524b
sections.translation: I am at peace with the dead .

sections.label: Section 2.2.525
sections.original: Scio . quíd modo igitur ? cur tanto opere extimueras ?
sections.translation: I recollect . Why then were you so dreadfully alarmed just now ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.526
sections.original: Nil me curassis , inquam , ego mihi providero :
sections.translation: Have no care for me , I tell you ; I ’ ll see to myself . You , as you have begun to do , fly as quick as ever you can ; Hercules , too [*] , you will invoke .

sections.label: Section 2.2.528b
sections.translation: Hercules , I do invoke thee ! ( Runs off .)

sections.label: Section 2.2.529
sections.original: Et ego — tibi hodie ut det , senex , magnum malum .
sections.translation: And I , as well , old fellow , that ; this day he ’ ll send some heavy mishap upon you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.530
sections.original: pro di immortales , obsecro vestram fidem ,
sections.translation: O ye immortal Gods , I do implore your aid . Plague on it ! what a mess I have got into to-day .

sections.label: Section 3.1.532
sections.translation: I never knew any year worse for money upon interest , than this year has turned out to me . From morning even until night , I spend my time in the Forum ;

sections.label: Section 3.1.535
sections.translation: I cannot lend out a coin of silver to any one . ( Enter TRANIO .)

sections.label: Section 3.1.536
sections.original: Nunc pol ego perii plane in perpetuom modum .
sections.translation: Now , faith , I am clearly undone in an everlasting way ! The Banker ’ s here who found the money with which his mistress was bought . The matter ’ s all out , unless I meet him a bit beforehand ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.540
sections.original: ne hóc senex resciscat . ibo huic obviam .
sections.translation: so that the old man may not at present come to know of this . I ’ ll go meet him . But ( seeing THEUROPIDES ) I wonder why he has so soon betaken himself homeward again . I ’ m afraid that he has heard something about this affair . I ’ ll meet him , and accost him . But how dreadfully frightened I am ! Nothing is more wretched than the mind of a man with a guilty conscience ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.545
sections.original: sicut me † habet . verum utut res sese habet ,
sections.translation: such as possesses myself . But however this matter turns out , I ’ ll proceed to perplex it still further : so does this affair require .

sections.label: Section 3.1.547
sections.translation: Whence come you ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.547b
sections.original: Conveni illum unde hasce aedis emeram .
sections.translation: I met that person from whom I bought this house .

sections.label: Section 3.1.548
sections.original: Numquid dixisti de illo quod dixi tibi ?
sections.translation: Did you tell him anything about that which I was telling you ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.549
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I certainly told him everything .

sections.label: Section 3.1.549b
sections.translation: Woe to unfortunate me !

sections.label: Section 3.1.550
sections.original: metuo ne techinae meae perpetuo perierint .
sections.translation: I ’ m afraid that my schemes are everlastingly undone !

sections.label: Section 3.1.551
sections.translation: What is it you are saying to yourself ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.551b
sections.translation: Why nothing . But tell me , prithee , did you really tell him ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.552b
sections.translation: I told him everything in its order , I tell you .

sections.label: Section 3.1.553
sections.translation: Does he , then , confess about the guest ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.553b
sections.translation: Why no ; he utterly denies it .

sections.label: Section 3.1.554
sections.translation: Does he deny it ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.554b
sections.translation: Do you ask me again ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.555b
sections.translation: I should tell you if he had confessed it . What now are you of opinion ought to be done ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.556b
sections.translation: What is my opinion ? By my troth , I beg of you , appoint an arbitrator together with him ; but take you care that you appoint one who will believe me ; you ’ ll overcome him as easily as a fox eats a pear [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.1.560
sections.translation: But see , here ’ s Tranio , the servant of Philolaches , people who pay me neither interest nor principal on my money .

sections.label: Section 3.1.562
sections.translation: Whither are you betaking yourself ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.562b
sections.original: Nec quoquam abeo . né ego sum miser ,
sections.translation: I ’ m going no whither . ( Aside .) For sure , I am a wretch , a rascal , one born with all the Gods my foes ! He ’ ll now be accosting me in the old man ’ s presence . Assuredly , I am a wretched man ;

sections.label: Section 3.1.565
sections.original: ita et hinc et illinc mi exhibent negotium .
sections.translation: in such a fashion both this way and that do they find business for me . But I ’ ll make haste and accost him . ( Moves towards the BANKER .)

sections.label: Section 3.1.566b
sections.original: Hic ad me it , salvos sum ,
sections.translation: He ’ s coming towards me . I ’ m all right I ’ ve some hopes of my money ;

sections.label: Section 3.1.567b
sections.translation: he ’ s smiling .

sections.label: Section 3.1.568
sections.translation: The fellow ’ s deceived . [*] ( To the BANKER .) I heartily bid you hail , my friend Saturides [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.1.569
sections.original: Salve et tu . quid de argentost ?
sections.translation: And hail to you . What about the money ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.569b
sections.translation: Be off with you , will you , you brute .

sections.label: Section 3.1.570
sections.translation: Directly you come , you commence the attack [*] against me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.571
sections.translation: This fellow ’ s empty-handed .

sections.label: Section 3.1.571b
sections.translation: This fellow ’ s surely a conjurer .

sections.label: Section 3.1.572
sections.translation: But why don ’ t you put an end to this trifling ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.572b
sections.translation: Tell me , then , what it is you want .

sections.label: Section 3.1.573
sections.translation: Where is Philolaches ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.573b
sections.translation: You never could have met me more opportunely than you have met me .

sections.label: Section 3.1.575
sections.translation: How ’ s that ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.575b
sections.translation: Step this way .

sections.label: Section 3.1.575c
sections.translation: Why isn ’ t the money repaid me ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.576
sections.original: Scio té bona esse voce , ne clama nimis .
sections.translation: I know that you have a good voice ; don ’ t bawl out so loud .

sections.label: Section 3.1.577
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I certainly shall bawl out .

sections.label: Section 3.1.577b
sections.translation: O do humour me now .

sections.label: Section 3.1.578
sections.original: Quid tibi ego morem vis geram ?
sections.translation: What do you want me to humour you in ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.578b
sections.translation: Prithee , be off hence home .

sections.label: Section 3.1.579
sections.translation: Be off ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.579b
sections.translation: Return here about mid-day .

sections.label: Section 3.1.580
sections.translation: Will the interest be paid then ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.580b
sections.translation: It will be paid . Be off .

sections.label: Section 3.1.581
sections.original: Quid ego huc recursem aut operam sumam aut conteram ?
sections.translation: Why should I run to and fro here , or use or waste my pains ? What if I remain here until mid-day in preference ?
